mnemonics: DOM Doctor: Use 'textContent' for a safe checkup (text only), 'innerHTML' for surgery (new HTML elements).
Attribute Ambulance: Remember the siren sound 'setAttribute' to change an element's properties on the fly.
Query & Conquer: You must first 'querySelector' an element to command it to update.
keyPoints: Use .textContent to safely update only the text within an element, shielding against XSS attacks.
Use .innerHTML to update both text and HTML markup inside an element, but be cautious of security risks.
Use .setAttribute(attrName, value) or direct property access (e.g., .src, .id) to update an element's attributes.
commonMistakes: Using innerHTML when only updating text, which is less performant and poses a security risk.
Forgetting to first select the element with querySelector or getElementById before trying to update it.
Confusing when to use a method (setAttribute) vs. directly setting a property (element.id = 'newID').
